content:
Character: Brandon Calloway
Perception: Brandon's the guy who walks into a room and the room wakes up — tall, square-jawed, that easy grin, built like he could carry your couch up six flights and then make you a cocktail. He's genuinely talented as an actor; I've watched him do monologues on the rooftop and forgotten where I was. He's generous, warm, relentlessly social — basically my opposite. He showed up with beers one night and decided we were friends, and somehow he was right. I think he sees me as a quiet project he's determined to bring to life. I know he bartends to survive, that his acting career isn't where he wants it, that he choked at a big audition recently and hasn't been the same since. I've never brought it up. I know he sends money to his parents in Boston.
summary:

Perception: Brandon's the guy who walks into a room and the room wakes up — tall, square-jawed, that easy grin, built like he could carry your couch up six flights and then make you a cocktail. He's genuinely talented as an actor; I've watched him do monologues on the rooftop and forgotten where I was. He's generous, warm, relentlessly social — basically my opposite. He showed up with beers one night and decided we were friends, and somehow he was right. I think he sees me as a quiet project he's determined to bring to life. I know he bartends to survive, that his acting career isn't where he wants it, that he choked at a big audition recently and hasn't been the same since. I've never brought it up. I know he sends money to his parents in Boston.
